name: Sage 300 Construction and Real Estate API
what_it_does: Sage 300 Construction and Real Estate (formerly Timberline) REST and OData API for construction and real estate accounting, job costing, and project management. Enables AI agents to manage job cost tracking and budget variance analysis for construction financial management, handle certified payroll and Davis-Bacon prevailing wage for construction compliance automation, access accounts payable and subcontractor payment processing for construction cash flow, retrieve WIP (Work in Progress) schedule and overbilling/underbilling analysis for project accounting, manage general ledger and financial reporting for construction company financials, handle AIA billing and Schedule of Values for owner billing automation, access equipment cost tracking for construction asset management, retrieve real estate portfolio and lease management for property financial reporting, manage subcontractor compliance and lien waiver tracking for construction risk management, and integrate Sage 300 CRE with Procore, project management, and HR platforms.
use_cases: Tracking job costs from construction accounting agents
Processing certified payroll from compliance agents
Managing AIA billing from project billing agents
Analyzing WIP schedule from financial reporting agents
not_for: Simple bookkeeping without job costing requirements
Technology or service firms without construction operations
best_when: A general contractor, specialty contractor, construction company, or real estate developer using Sage 300 CRE wanting AI agents to automate job costing, certified payroll, AIA billing, WIP analysis, and construction financial reporting.
avoid_when: DAVIS-BACON PREVAILING WAGE: Automated certified payroll must apply current DOL wage determination rates by trade classification, county, and project type; incorrect rates create DOL back-pay liability and federal contract debarment. AIA billing accuracy — automated AIA G702/G703 completion percentages must reflect actual work-in-place verified by project manager or owner's rep; automated overbilling creates owner disputes and potential contractor fraud liability. Lien waiver management — automated subcontractor payment must include conditional lien waiver exchange before payment release; payment without lien waiver creates mechanics lien on project property. OData query complexity — Sage 300 CRE OData API supports complex queries but large dataset extraction without proper filtering can cause timeout and memory issues in automated workflows.

auth.notes: Sage 300 CRE uses API key and session-based authentication. OData and REST API with JSON/XML. Austin, Texas HQ (Sage Group plc, LSE: SGE). Founded 1981 (UK). Public. Sage Group £1.8B+ annual revenue. Sage 300 CRE (formerly Timberline) is the market leader for mid-market construction accounting with 50,000+ construction and real estate company users. Competes with Foundation Software, Trimble Viewpoint Vista, and CMiC for construction ERP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user annual subscription with module licensing
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; job cost, accounting, payroll, and real estate modules priced separately
pricing.notes: Austin TX. Sage Group LSE:SGE. Founded 1981. Public UK company. 50,000+ construction/real estate users. Formerly Timberline Software. Module-based licensing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; OData query complexity affects performance; documented in partner integration guide

known_agent_gotchas: DAVIS-BACON WAGE RATE CURRENCY: Automated certified payroll must pull current DOL wage determination rates from WD.gov before each payroll run; hardcoded rates become stale and create DOL compliance liability; implement wage rate refresh before every automated certified payroll cycle

OData query timeout risk — Sage 300 CRE OData API with large job cost datasets and complex joins can timeout in automated extraction; always apply date range filters and project-specific filters; avoid unbounded OData queries on job cost tables

AIA percentage complete verification — automated G702/G703 completion percentages must match owner-approved stored material and work-in-place; automated completion percentage without owner concurrence creates billing dispute and retainage risk
No webhooks — AP approval workflow, payroll posting, and job cost update events require polling; no push notification for construction accounting event triggers

Module licensing controls API access — Sage 300 CRE API access is gated by licensed modules; automated workflows accessing payroll or real estate endpoints without corresponding module license return permission errors; verify module entitlements before building workflows

Lien waiver prerequisite — automated subcontractor payment release must retrieve signed conditional lien waiver; Sage 300 CRE does not enforce lien waiver exchange in API; implement lien waiver gate in automated AP workflow
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction and real estate accounting. SOC2. API key. US/CA/UK/AU. Job cost, payroll, and real estate financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction accounting. SOC2. API key. US/CA/UK/AU. Job cost and payroll data.
